---
title: "testing"
output: html_document
date: "2025-08-18"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "~/projects/021_ltee_models/NeutralKaryotypes/")
```



```{r}
require(DBI)
require(RMariaDB)
source("R/db_utils.R")
dbvars <- load_db_vars("db_creds.txt")
db <- dbConnect(
    MariaDB(),
    host = dbvars["HOST"],
    user = dbvars["USER"],
    password = dbvars["PASSWORD"],
    dbname = dbvars["DBNAME"]
)

x <- dbGetQuery(db, "SELECT * FROM Passaging")
m <- dbGetQuery(db,"SELECT * FROM Media")
db_col <- collapse_db(x) 

saveRDS(db_col,"core_data/db_col.Rds")
```

```{r}
db_col <- readRDS("core_data/db_col.Rds")
ids <- unique(readLines("core_data/karyotyped_samples.txt"))

if(!file.exists("core_data/karyotypes.Rds")){
  karyotypes <- get_karyotyping(ids)
  saveRDS(karyotypes,"core_data/karyotypes.Rds")
}


ids <- intersect(ids, db_col$samples$samples)

sample_map <- db_col$samples[match(ids, db_col$samples$samples), , drop = FALSE]
sample_map <- unique(sample_map[, c("samples", "passage_id")])
sample_map <- sample_map[order(sample_map$passage_id, sample_map$samples), ] 
print(sample_map)

passaging_tbl <- db_col$passaging
parent_of <- setNames(passaging_tbl$passage_from, passaging_tbl$passage_id)
unmapped_passage_ids <- setdiff(unique(sample_map$passage_id), passaging_tbl$passage_id)
if (length(unmapped_passage_ids) > 0) {
  warning(length(unmapped_passage_ids),
          " sampled passage_id values are absent from db_col$passaging and will be skipped.")
}

find_passage_ancestors <- function(passage_id, parent_map) {
  out <- character()
  cur <- passage_id
  while (!is.null(cur) && !is.na(cur)) {
    out <- c(cur, out)
    if (!(cur %in% names(parent_map))) break
    cur <- unname(parent_map[cur])
  }
  out
}

common_path_prefix <- function(paths) {
  if (!length(paths)) return(character())
  min_len <- min(vapply(paths, length, integer(1)))
  prefix <- character()
  for (i in seq_len(min_len)) {
    vals <- vapply(paths, `[`, character(1), i)
    if (length(unique(vals)) != 1) break
    prefix <- c(prefix, vals[1])
  }
  prefix
}

trim_path_to_node <- function(path, node_id) {
  node_idx <- match(node_id, path)
  if (is.na(node_idx)) {
    stop("Node ", node_id, " was not present in the supplied path.")
  }
  path[node_idx:length(path)]
}

sampled_passage_ids <- setdiff(unique(sample_map$passage_id), unmapped_passage_ids)
passage_paths <- setNames(
  lapply(sampled_passage_ids, find_passage_ancestors, parent_map = parent_of),
  sampled_passage_ids
)
root_id <- vapply(passage_paths, function(path) path[1], character(1))

connected_passage_sets <- split(sampled_passage_ids, root_id)
connected_sample_sets <- lapply(connected_passage_sets, function(pids) {
  sample_map$samples[sample_map$passage_id %in% pids]
})

build_component_tree <- function(component_passage_ids, path_map, passaging_tbl, sample_map) {
  component_paths <- path_map[component_passage_ids]
  common_prefix <- common_path_prefix(component_paths)
  if (!length(common_prefix)) {
    stop("Could not determine a shared ancestry path for component.")
  }

  mrca_id <- tail(common_prefix, 1)
  trimmed_paths <- lapply(component_paths, trim_path_to_node, node_id = mrca_id)
  subtree_nodes <- sort(unique(unlist(trimmed_paths)))
  subtree_edges <- passaging_tbl[
    passaging_tbl$passage_id %in% subtree_nodes,
    c("passage_id", "passage_from"),
    drop = FALSE
  ]
  subtree_edges$passage_from[!(subtree_edges$passage_from %in% subtree_nodes)] <- NA_character_
  subtree_samples <- sample_map[sample_map$passage_id %in% subtree_nodes, , drop = FALSE]

  list(
    component_root_id = common_prefix[1],
    mrca_id = mrca_id,
    mrca_depth = length(common_prefix),
    sampled_passage_ids = sort(component_passage_ids),
    sampled_ids = sort(unique(subtree_samples$samples[subtree_samples$passage_id %in% component_passage_ids])),
    tree_passage_ids = subtree_nodes,
    tree_edges = subtree_edges[order(subtree_edges$passage_id), , drop = FALSE],
    tree_samples = subtree_samples[order(subtree_samples$passage_id, subtree_samples$samples), , drop = FALSE],
    trimmed_paths = trimmed_paths
  )
}

connected_trees <- lapply(connected_passage_sets, build_component_tree,
                          path_map = passage_paths,
                          passaging_tbl = passaging_tbl,
                          sample_map = sample_map)

component_summary <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(connected_trees), function(i) {
  tree <- connected_trees[[i]]
  data.frame(
    component = names(connected_trees)[i],
    component_root_id = tree$component_root_id,
    mrca_id = tree$mrca_id,
    trimmed_pre_mrca = tree$component_root_id != tree$mrca_id,
    mrca_depth = tree$mrca_depth,
    n_sampled_passages = length(tree$sampled_passage_ids),
    n_sampled_ids = length(tree$sampled_ids),
    n_tree_passages = length(tree$tree_passage_ids),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))
print(component_summary)

if (any(component_summary$trimmed_pre_mrca)) {
  message("Trimmed pre-MRCA ancestry from ",
          sum(component_summary$trimmed_pre_mrca),
          " connected tree(s).")
  print(component_summary[component_summary$trimmed_pre_mrca, c(
    "component", "component_root_id", "mrca_id", "mrca_depth",
    "n_sampled_passages", "n_tree_passages"
  )])
}

# Example access:
# connected_trees[[1]]$tree_edges
# connected_trees[[1]]$tree_samples
```
At this point we need to attach media information to our dataset. 

```{r}
all_ids <- unlist(lapply(connected_trees,"[[","tree_passage_ids"))

## For these experiments ideally media would be consistent across a passage, but of course we know it won't be so better check:
media_ids <- lapply(all_ids,function(id) db_col$samples$media_id[db_col$samples$passage_id%in%id])
medias_per_passage <- sapply(media_ids,function(id) length(unique(id))) 
table(medias_per_passage) 
## Do something like this to manually inspect:
## db_col$sample[db_col$sample$passage_id%in%all_ids[medias_per_passage>1],]
## or:
## db_col$sample[db_col$sample$passage_id%in%all_ids[which(medias_per_passage>1)[1]],"media_id"]
## looks like there is a mix between potential data entry issues and genuinely varyig media throughout passage.
## safest thing to do IMO is to take most common media id per passage and assign that to he passage id.

media_ids <- sapply(media_ids,function(i){
  agg <- table(i)
  names(agg)[which.max(agg)]
})

m_col <- build_media_col(unique(media_ids),m)

## it's actually painful looking at m_col, asking oneself questions like "when EnergySource2=='NEAA' and EnergySource2_pct==1, did they mean 100% or is it really 1%?". Sigh. I'll make sure that jumps out on any plots.

## I think the media table is so resistant to robust machine parsing that I have no choice but to define filters manually... 
filters <- list(!is.na(m_col$EnergySource2) & m_col$EnergySource2_pct<100, ## mostly seems to identify phosphate starvation
                m_col$oxygen_pct<20.5, ##obvious
                m_col$EnergySource=="L_glutamine" & m_col$EnergySource_nM<2000000 &!is.na(m_col$EnergySource_nM),
                m_col$EnergySource=="Glucose" & m_col$EnergySource_nM<1110150&!is.na(m_col$EnergySource_nM))
filters <- do.call(cbind,filters)                
condition <- apply(filters,1,function(i){
  if(sum(i)==0) return("control")
  deprivations <- c("phosphate","oxygen","glutamine","glucose")[i]
  paste(deprivations,collapse="_")
})

m_col$condition <- condition

passage_media <- data.frame(
  passage_id = all_ids,
  media_id = unname(media_ids),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
passage_media$condition <- m_col$condition[match(passage_media$media_id, m_col$media_id)]

connected_trees <- lapply(connected_trees, function(tree) {
  tree$tree_conditions <- passage_media[
    match(tree$tree_passage_ids, passage_media$passage_id),
    ,
    drop = FALSE
  ]
  tree$tree_conditions <- tree$tree_conditions[order(tree$tree_conditions$passage_id), , drop = FALSE]
  tree
})

```


```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
source("R/plot_utils.R")

tree_plots <- lapply(seq_along(connected_trees), function(i) {
  plot_passage_tree(
    connected_trees[[i]],
    component_name = names(connected_trees)[i]
  )
})
names(tree_plots) <- names(connected_trees)

invisible(lapply(tree_plots, print))

```

```{r}
source("R/db_utils.R")
source("R/estimate_pmis.R")
library(ggplot2)

karyotypes <- readRDS("core_data/karyotypes.Rds")
simulation_intervals <- build_simulation_intervals(connected_trees, karyotypes)
grouped_intervals <- group_simulation_intervals(simulation_intervals)
group_fit_df <- estimate_group_pmis_grid(
  grouped_intervals,
  pwgd_grid = c(0,1e-3,1e-2,0.05)
)


# The Markov likelihood below now allows state 0. In this model, copy number 0
# is absorbing because the per-chromosome event rate is x * p_mis, so once a
# chromosome is lost there is no route back. Over long intervals or at high
# p_mis this can concentrate mass on zero-copy states, implying unrealistically
# many cells eventually lose that chromosome permanently.
zero_copy_summary <- do.call(rbind, lapply(simulation_intervals, function(interval) {
  start_zero <- which(colSums(interval$start_karyotype == 0, na.rm = TRUE) > 0)
  end_zero <- which(colSums(interval$end_karyotype == 0, na.rm = TRUE) > 0)
  all_zero_chr <- sort(unique(c(start_zero, end_zero)))
  if (!length(all_zero_chr)) return(NULL)

  zero_rows <- unique(c(
    rownames(interval$start_karyotype)[rowSums(interval$start_karyotype == 0, na.rm = TRUE) > 0],
    rownames(interval$end_karyotype)[rowSums(interval$end_karyotype == 0, na.rm = TRUE) > 0]
  ))

  data.frame(
    connected_tree = interval$connected_tree,
    start = interval$start,
    end = interval$end,
    condition = interval$condition,
    zero_copy_chromosomes = paste0("chr", all_zero_chr, collapse = ", "),
    zero_copy_samples = paste(zero_rows, collapse = ", "),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))
print(zero_copy_summary)

best_group_pmis <- do.call(rbind, lapply(split(group_fit_df, group_fit_df$group_id), function(df) {
  df[which.min(df$negll), c("group_id", "pmis", "pwgd", "negll", "n_members")]
}))
rownames(best_group_pmis) <- NULL
print(best_group_pmis)

best_by_pwgd <- do.call(rbind, lapply(split(group_fit_df, list(group_fit_df$group_id, group_fit_df$pwgd), drop = TRUE), function(df) {
  df[which.min(df$negll), c("group_id", "pmis", "pwgd", "negll", "n_members")]
}))
rownames(best_by_pwgd) <- NULL
print(best_by_pwgd)

ploidy_pred_diag <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(best_by_pwgd)), function(i) {
  group_id <- best_by_pwgd$group_id[i]
  pred_df <- predict_group_ploidy_distribution(
    grouped_intervals[[group_id]],
    p_mis = best_by_pwgd$pmis[i],
    p_wgd = best_by_pwgd$pwgd[i]
  )

  pred_df <- aggregate(
    list(value = pred_df$prob),
    by = list(
      group_id = rep(group_id, nrow(pred_df)),
      pwgd = rep(best_by_pwgd$pwgd[i], nrow(pred_df)),
      ploidy = pred_df$ploidy
    ),
    sum
  )
  pred_df$type <- "predicted"
  pred_df
}))

ploidy_obs_diag <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(grouped_intervals), function(group_id) {
  obs_df <- observed_group_ploidy_distribution(grouped_intervals[[group_id]])
  obs_df <- aggregate(
    list(value = rep(1, nrow(obs_df))),
    by = list(group_id = rep(group_id, nrow(obs_df)), ploidy = obs_df$ploidy),
    sum
  )
  obs_df$value <- obs_df$value / sum(obs_df$value)
  obs_df
}))

p_ploidy <- ggplot() +
  geom_line(
    data = ploidy_pred_diag,
    aes(x = ploidy, y = value, color = factor(pwgd), group = factor(pwgd))
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = ploidy_obs_diag,
    aes(x = ploidy, y = value),
    color = "black"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~group_id, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous("mean copy number across chr1:22") +
  scale_y_continuous("probability / relative frequency") +
  scale_color_discrete("pwgd") +
  ggtitle("Predicted ploidy distributions by pwgd level with observed endpoints")
p_ploidy

p <- ggplot(group_fit_df,aes(x=pmis,y=negll,color=factor(pwgd)))+
  facet_wrap(~group_id,scales="free")+
  geom_line()+
  scale_x_log10()
p
```

```{r}
forward_group_id <- best_group_pmis$group_id[7]
forward_fit <- best_group_pmis[best_group_pmis$group_id == forward_group_id, ][7, ]

forward_runs <- simulate_group_forward(
  grouped_intervals[[forward_group_id]],
  fit_row = forward_fit,
  bottleneck_size = 1000,
  expansion_factor = 16,
  n_reps = 2,
  record_every = 50
)

forward_summary <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(forward_runs), function(interval_idx) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(forward_runs[[interval_idx]]), function(rep_idx) {
    sim <- forward_runs[[interval_idx]][[rep_idx]]
    final_ploidy <- if (nrow(sim$final_matrix)) rowMeans(sim$final_matrix) else NA_real_
    wtst <- wasserstein_permutation_test(
      observed_matrix = grouped_intervals[[forward_group_id]][[interval_idx]]$end_karyotype,
      simulated_matrix = sim$final_matrix,
      n_perm = 20,
      seed = 10000 + interval_idx * 100 + rep_idx
    )
    data.frame(
      group_id = forward_group_id,
      interval_idx = interval_idx,
      rep_idx = rep_idx,
      n_final_cells = nrow(sim$final_matrix),
      mean_ploidy = mean(final_ploidy, na.rm = TRUE),
      frac_zero_chr = mean(sim$final_matrix == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
      wasserstein = wtst$statistic,
      p_value = wtst$p_value,
      passes_wasserstein = is.finite(wtst$p_value) & wtst$p_value > 0.05,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
}))
print(forward_summary)

forward_group_summary <- merge(
  aggregate(
    list(
      median_wasserstein = forward_summary$wasserstein,
      median_p_value = forward_summary$p_value
    ),
    by = list(group_id = forward_summary$group_id, interval_idx = forward_summary$interval_idx),
    FUN = median
  ),
  aggregate(
    list(pass_rate = as.numeric(forward_summary$passes_wasserstein)),
    by = list(group_id = forward_summary$group_id, interval_idx = forward_summary$interval_idx),
    FUN = mean
  ),
  by = c("group_id", "interval_idx")
)
print(forward_group_summary)

forward_ploidy <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(forward_runs), function(interval_idx) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(forward_runs[[interval_idx]]), function(rep_idx) {
    sim <- forward_runs[[interval_idx]][[rep_idx]]
    if (!nrow(sim$final_matrix)) return(NULL)
    data.frame(
      interval_idx = interval_idx,
      rep_idx = rep_idx,
      ploidy = rowMeans(sim$final_matrix),
      type = "simulated",
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
}))

observed_forward_ploidy <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(grouped_intervals[[forward_group_id]]), function(interval_idx) {
  data.frame(
    interval_idx = interval_idx,
    rep_idx = NA_integer_,
    ploidy = rowMeans(grouped_intervals[[forward_group_id]][[interval_idx]]$end_karyotype),
    type = "observed",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

forward_ploidy_df <- rbind(forward_ploidy, observed_forward_ploidy)
p_forward <- ggplot(forward_ploidy_df, aes(x = ploidy, color = type, fill = type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~interval_idx, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous("mean copy number across chr1:22") +
  ggtitle(paste("Forward simulation ploidy check:", forward_group_id))
p_forward
```
